name: Lemon Meringue Pie from Mccall's Cooking Scho
recipeInstructions:
On lightly floured pastry cloth, roll the pastry to an 11-inch circle. Fold the pastry in half and carefully transfer it to a 9-inch pie plate. Unfold; fit into a pie plate, pressing gently toward the center. Fold the edge of the crust under and press into an upright rim. Crimp the edge decoratively using your thumb and forefinger. Refrigerate for 1/2 hour.
Preheat the oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit.
Prick the entire surface evenly with a fork. Bake until golden-brown, about 8 to 10 minutes. Cool on a rack.
In a medium saucepan, combine cornstarch, flour, sugar, and salt. Gradually add water, stirring until smooth. Bring to boiling, stirring occasionally; boil for 1 minute until shiny and translucent. Stir in some of the hot mixture into the yolks. Pour back into the hot mixture and stir to blend. Return to heat and cook over low heat for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat and stir in lemon juice, lemon peel, and butter. Pour into the pie shell.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium bowl, beat egg whites with cream of tartar until frothy. Gradually beat in sugar, about 1/2 cup at a time, beating after each addition. Beat at high speed until stiff peaks form when the beater is slowly raised. Spread meringue over the lemon filling, carefully sealing to the edge of the crust and swirling the top decoratively. Bake until the meringue is golden-brown, about 7 to 9 minutes. Let cool completely on a rack for 2 to 3 hours.
Cut with a wet knife.
